# wydoujin API

> wydoujin is a self-hosted, single-user reading server for a doujin/manga library.
> This file documents the machine API that lets an LLM agent help organize the library:
> search works, read the tag vocabulary, and edit tags, series, and scans.

All organize operations are database-only. They never touch the read-only library
files on disk. A work's identity is its `content_hash`, so edits survive renames.

## Base URL

`{APP_URL}/api/v1` ‚Äî for example `http://localhost/api/v1`.

## Authentication

Send the server-configured token (set in the deployment's `.env` as `API_TOKEN`) on
every request, as either header:

- `Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN>`
- `X-Api-Token: <API_TOKEN>`

Also send `Accept: application/json` (and `Content-Type: application/json` for bodies).

Responses:
- `401 Unauthenticated` ‚Äî token missing or wrong.
- `503 API disabled` ‚Äî no `API_TOKEN` is configured on the server.
- `422` ‚Äî validation or an invariant violation (the JSON `message` explains).
- `404` ‚Äî unknown id.

The token is configured server-side only and is never returned by the API.

## Data model

- **mangaka** ‚Äî one per top-level library folder (the artist/circle the folder is named for).
- **series** ‚Äî a per-mangaka grouping of works (series never cross mangaka).
- **work** ‚Äî one per `.zip`. Metadata lives in tags, not columns.
- **tag** ‚Äî a normalized `type` + `value`. Types: `circle`, `parody`, `event`, `author`,
  `flag` (all auto-derived from the filename) and `theme` (manual-only).
- Editing a work's tags sets `tags_locked` (the scanner won't re-derive it); editing a
  work's series sets `series_locked` (auto-detection won't undo it). These make your
  changes durable across rescans.

## Read endpoints

- `GET /works` ‚Äî search/list works. Query params:
  - `q` ‚Äî title search.
  - `circle[]`, `parody[]`, `event[]`, `author[]`, `flag[]`, `theme[]` ‚Äî facet filters
    (OR within a type, AND across types). Values are raw (often Japanese), URL-encoded.
  - `mangaka` ‚Äî id or slug. `series` ‚Äî id.
  - `untagged=1` ‚Äî only works with no tags. `tags_locked=1` ‚Äî only locked works.
  - `missing=1` ‚Äî only works swept as missing.
  - `page`, `per_page` (max 100).
- `GET /works/{id}` ‚Äî one work in full (tags grouped by type, mangaka, series, progress).
- `GET /mangaka` ‚Äî list mangaka with work/series counts.
- `GET /mangaka/{id}` ‚Äî a mangaka with its series and works.
- `GET /series/{id}` ‚Äî a series with its works.
- `GET /tags` ‚Äî the canonical tag vocabulary. Params: `type`, `q`. Each has `works_count`.
- `GET /facets` ‚Äî dynamic facet counts for the same filters as `GET /works`.

## Write endpoints

Per-work tags (each returns the updated work):
- `POST /works/{id}/tags` ‚Äî attach one tag. Body: `{ "type": "theme", "value": "netorare" }`.
- `PUT /works/{id}/tags` ‚Äî replace the whole set. Body: `{ "tags": [ {"type","value"}, ... ] }`
  (an empty array clears all tags; the work stays locked).
- `DELETE /works/{id}/tags` ‚Äî detach. Body: `{ "tag_id": 12 }` or `{ "type", "value" }`.
- `POST /works/{id}/tags/reset` ‚Äî clear the lock and re-derive tags from the filename.

Bulk tags (one tag across many works):
- `POST /tags/attach` ‚Äî Body: `{ "type", "value", "work_ids": [1,2,3] }`.
- `POST /tags/detach` ‚Äî Body: `{ "type", "value", "work_ids": [1,2,3] }` (lenient: skips
  works that don't carry the tag).

Global tags (tidy the vocabulary; durable across rescans via a merge-alias):
- `PATCH /tags/{id}` ‚Äî rename. Body: `{ "value": "NTR" }`. Renaming onto an existing value merges.
- `POST /tags/{id}/merge` ‚Äî merge this tag into another. Body: `{ "into_id": 9 }`.

Series (works in a group must all belong to one mangaka):
- `POST /series` ‚Äî group works into a new series. Body: `{ "work_ids": [1,2], "name": "..." }`.
- `POST /series/{id}/works` ‚Äî add works. Body: `{ "work_ids": [3] }`.
- `DELETE /series/works` ‚Äî ungroup works (back to standalone). Body: `{ "work_ids": [1] }`.
- `PATCH /series/{id}` ‚Äî rename. Body: `{ "name": "..." }`.

Maintenance:
- `POST /scan` ‚Äî trigger a full library scan (dedupes if one is already running).
- `GET /scan` ‚Äî latest scan status.
- `POST /works/{id}/rescan` ‚Äî re-inspect a single work.

## Suggested workflow

1. `GET /works?untagged=1&mangaka=...` ‚Äî find unorganized works.
2. `GET /tags?type=theme&q=...` ‚Äî check the existing vocabulary before inventing values.
3. `POST /tags/attach` ‚Äî bulk-tag the matching works.
4. `POST /series` ‚Äî group works that belong to one series.
5. `PATCH /tags/{id}` or `POST /tags/{id}/merge` ‚Äî consolidate duplicate/variant tags.

Because per-work edits set `tags_locked` and series edits set `series_locked`, and a
rename/merge leaves a normalizing alias, your changes persist through future scans.
